channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: This great free tool brings Pixel-quality image sharpening to any device
item.description: It really is mind-blowing how much incredible stuff we can do with images these days. ’Twasn’t long ago, after all, that advanced image adjustments required pricey desktop-computer software and super-specific knowledge.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Hackers find a way around built-in Windows protections
item.description: If not properly managed, Windows Defender Application Control, a security feature with Windows, could be a potential security vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reacher season 4: everything we know so far about the hit Prime Video show’s return
item.description: Here's everything we know about Reacher season 4, which beings filming in mid-2025, ahead of the hit Prime Video show's return.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The next big AI shift in media? Turning news into a two-way conversation
item.description: When people talk about how AI might reshape media, the term “hyper-personalization” comes up a lot. In broad terms, it means that AI can tailor the experience around your preferences—assuming it has enough data about you.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: 5 things to know about Meta’s upcoming FTC trial
item.description: Meta is set to face off against the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) on Monday in an antitrust trial that could force the social media giant to divest itself of Instagram and WhatsApp. The closely watched trial carries high stakes for Meta’s $1.3 trillion market value.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: DHS employees consider workforce transition amid major uncertainty
item.description: Staff across DHS, including at CISA and FEMA, are considering deferred resignation or other offers amid reports that their agencies will be targeted for cuts. The post DHS employees consider workforce transition amid major uncertainty first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Google lays off hundreds, impacting Android and Pixel teams
item.description: Alphabet’s Google laid off hundreds of employees in its platforms and devices unit, The Information reported on Friday, citing a person with direct knowledge of the situation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: The Certification Authority Browser Forum Votes to Radically Slash Certificate Lifespan
item.description: The Certification Authority Browser Forum votes to radically slash the lifespan of website certificates, citing improved web security. The change will be phased in gradually through March 2029.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Social Security Administration 'will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The shift comes as communications staff at the agency has dwindled due to reassignments in front-facing roles at field offices across the country.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Musk 'most unlikeable person' in US: House Democrat
item.description: Rep. Robert Garcia (D-Calif.) called Elon Musk the “most unlikeable person” in the U.S. during a Thursday speech in support of his party’s push to oust the tech giant from his White House role by May 30.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Theranos fraudster Elizabeth Holmes keeps fighting, claims appeal judges were confused, illogical and factually mistaken
item.description: The jury in Holmes’ trial heard evidence that she doctored internal Theranos documents and that she and Theranos had falsely suggested to investors that her machines were in battlefield use.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Waymo robotaxis make up 20% of Uber rides in Austin, data shows
item.description: Waymo robotaxis made up about 20% of rides offered by Uber Technologies Inc. in Austin in the last week of March, underscoring how consumers have warmed to the autonomous service since the companies launched a partnership early last month.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What is data fabric? How it offers a unified view of your data
item.description: What is data fabric? Data fabric is a type of architecture that aims to provide unified access to the data stored in various places across your organization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s latest attempt to persuade upgrades to Windows 11 falls spectacularly flat on its face
item.description: “You’ve heard the buzz about Windows 11, but is it worth the upgrade?” Microsoft asks. And going by this new article, it isn’t really.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Russian hackers hit military mission in Ukraine with info-stealing malware on external drives
item.description: GammaSteel infostealer found on infected devices belonging to a Western military operation in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects
item.description: Microsoft said on Wednesday it is "slowing or pausing" some of its data center projects, describing the move as a show of flexibility as the artificial intelligence (AI) industry evolves.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Hawley calls on Zuckerberg to appear before Congress following whistleblower testimony
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) called on Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg to testify before Congress after a former Facebook employee alleged the company repeatedly undermined U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple has few incentives to start making iPhones in US, despite Trump's trade war with China
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has been predicting its barrage of tariffs targeting China will push Apple into manufacturing the iPhone in the U.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google reaffirms $75B AI infra investment as cloud providers pursue divergent strategies
item.description: In a move closely watched by enterprise technology leaders, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai has reaffirmed Google’s commitment to spending $75 billion this year on AI infrastructure and data centers — weeks after Microsoft reportedly abandoned many of its data center projects.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Drones are the future of cybercrime
item.description: The Ukrainian military is teaching the world how to creatively use drones for battlefield offense and defense. Ukraine has built a vast arsenal of drones customized for precision strikes, reconnaissance, and kamikaze missions. But this innovation also extends to cybersecurity offense and defense.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: April update has broken Windows Hello for some devices but there’s a quick fix
item.description: A software hiccup has occurred with Windows Hello following this month’s security updates, one that could prevent users from logging in via facial recognition.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump SEC chair pick Paul Atkins confirmed by Senate
item.description: The Senate confirmed Paul Atkins as the next Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) leader. Atkins, a former Republican SEC commissioner, was greenlighted by the Senate in a 52-44 vote Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Meta helped build China’s DeepSeek: Whistleblower testimony
item.description: Former Meta executive Sarah Wynn-Williams is set to testify before the US Senate Judiciary Committee on Wednesday, revealing how the company’s AI model, Llama, played a critical role in accelerating China’s AI capabilities — particularly contributing to the rise of DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
